steps.title: Recall the radian-to-degree conversion factor
steps.content: To convert an angle from radians to degrees, multiply by the conversion factor $\frac{180^\circ}{\pi}$.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong conversion directions and factors
steps.content:
• Choice B multiplies by $\frac{\pi}{180^\circ}$, which converts degrees to radians (opposite direction). Eliminate.
  • Choice D multiplies by $\frac{360^\circ}{\pi}$, not the correct $\frac{180^\circ}{\pi}$. Eliminate.

steps.title: Identify the correct expression
steps.content: Choice C shows $-\frac{5\pi}{12} \cdot \frac{180^\circ}{\pi}$, which correctly multiplies the radian measure by the degree conversion factor. That matches choice C — C is correct.
